cards.back: A logical memory space that combines physical RAM and hard disk storage to provide a larger address space for running applications.
A technique used in virtual memory management to divide the physical RAM into smaller segments called pages, and transfer them to disk when needed.
The process of transferring pages from RAM to disk storage when the system runs low on physical RAM, and loading them back into RAM when needed.
Set of algorithms used to decide which pages to swap out of physical RAM when the system runs low on free space, such as LRU and FIFO.
A mechanism used to translate virtual addresses into physical addresses, allowing the operating system to manage the interaction between virtual memory and physical memory.
